824	# SECOND-HAND ATTRIBUTION. The defect the artifact verifier caught, and the most dangerous one here.
825	#
826	# A literature review's papers are FULL of other papers' numbers. Braganza (2021) contains the
827	# sentence "It is estimated that, globally, 326 million mostly-low-skilled jobs will be adversely
828	# affected by AI within 10 years (...)". That span is VERBATIM. Its figure IS in the span. It passes
829	# every gate this module has — and it is a lie, because the card says `source: Braganza (2021)` and
830	# the writer will render "Writing in the Journal of Business Research in 2021, Braganza et al. show
831	# that 326 million jobs will be adversely affected".
832	#
833	# BRAGANZA NEVER FOUND THAT. He cited it.
834	#
835	# A REAL number, in a REAL paper, bound to a source that did not produce it — assembled entirely
836	# from true particulars, and invisible to every span/number check. It is the same shape as the
837	# task-displacement/Bresnahan fabrication that already cost this project a turn. A card is evidence
838	# FROM its source; if the span hands the finding to someone else, the card's provenance is false.
839	THIRD_PARTY = re.compile(
840	    r'\[\d{1,3}\]'                                             # [34]
841	    r'|\(\s*[A-Z][A-Za-z-]+(?:\s+(?:et al\.?|and\s+[A-Z][A-Za-z-]+))?[,\s]+\(?(?:19|20)\d{2}'  # (Autor, 2003)
842	    r'|\b[A-Z][a-z]+\s+et\s+al\.'                              # Autor et al.
843	    r'|\baccording to\s+(?:the\s+)?[A-Z]'                      # According to the OECD
844	    r'|\b(?:prior|previous|earlier|existing)\s+(?:work|studies|research|literature|estimates)\b'
845	    r'|\bstudies\s+have\s+(?:shown|found|reported)\b'
846	    r'|\bit\s+is\s+(?:estimated|predicted|projected|forecast)\s+that\b'
847	    r'|\b(?:has|have)\s+(?:predicted|projected|estimated|forecast)\b'
848	    r'|\b(?:report|reports|reported|find|finds|found|show|shows|argue|argues|suggest|suggests)\s+'
849	    r'(?:that\s+)?(?:in|by)?\s*\[\d', re.I)
850	
851	# The paper speaking in its OWN voice. If the sentence is the paper's own result, a citation
852	# elsewhere in it is a data source, not an attribution of the finding.
853	FIRST_PERSON = re.compile(
854	    r'\b(?:we|our|us)\b|\bthis\s+(?:paper|study|article|research|analysis)\b'
855	    r'|\bthe\s+(?:present|current)\s+(?:study|paper|analysis)\b'
856	    r'|\bin\s+this\s+(?:paper|study|article)\b'
857	    r'|\b(?:table|figure|column|panel|model|specification)\s+\(?[0-9ivx]', re.I)
858	
859	# A forecast is not a measured effect. synthesis_contract.py: "Forecasts / predictions are always
860	# fabrication in a literature review." They are kept, but they are NOT counted as evidence of an
861	# effect and they are never labelled `estimate`.
862	PROJECTION = re.compile(
863	    r'\bwill\s+(?:be|have|create|destroy|displace|replace|affect|eliminate|reach|rise|fall|grow|lose)\b'
864	    r'|\b(?:by|before)\s+20[2-9]\d\b|\bis\s+(?:expected|projected|predicted|forecast)\s+to\b'
865	    r'|\b(?:predict|forecast|project)(?:s|ed|ion|ions)?\b|\bin\s+the\s+(?:next|coming)\s+\d+\s+years?\b'
866	    r'|\bwithin\s+\d+\s+years?\b|\bpotential(?:ly)?\s+(?:at\s+risk|automatable)\b', re.I)
867	
868	
869	# The findings-bearing verbs. A number in a sentence with one of these is far more likely a RESULT
870	# than a citation year or a page number.
871	RESULT_VERB = re.compile(
872	    r'\b(find|finds|found|estimat\w+|show|shows|showed|report\w*|observ\w+|estimate[sd]?|associat\w+|'
873	    r'predict\w*|suggest\w*|indicat\w+|imply|implies|demonstrat\w+|result\w*|conclude\w*|document\w*|'
874	    r'increas\w+|decreas\w+|declin\w+|reduc\w+|rais\w+|rose|fell|grew|correlat\w+|regress\w+|coefficient|'
875	    r'effect|impact|elasticit\w+|significant\w*|robust\w*|we\s+(?:find|estimate|show|observe|report))\b', re.I)
876	
877	# Bibliography rows, page furniture, journal boilerplate. These are DENSE with numbers and are not
878	# evidence. Count-chasing without this filter is how you print "202 numbers" of pure noise.
879	NOISE = re.compile(
880	    r'\bdoi\b|https?://|\bissn\b|\bisbn\b|\bvol\.?\s*\d+|\bno\.?\s*\d+\s*[,(]|\bpp?\.\s*\d+\s*[-–]\s*\d+'
881	    r'|\b\d{4};\s*\d+\s*\(|\(\d{4}\)\.\s|all rights reserved|copyright|©\s*\d{4}|downloaded from'
882	    r'|working paper (no|series)|electronic copy available|cookie|sign in|\bcrossref\b|\bpubmed\b'
883	    r'|jstor|elsevier b\.v\.|\bet al\.\s*\(\d{4}\)\.\s*[A-Z]', re.I)
884	
885	SENT_SPLIT = re.compile(r'(?<=[.!?])[\'")\]]?\s+(?=[A-Z0-9("\[•-])')
886	
887	
888	def sentences(text: str, base: int = 0) -> list[tuple[int, int, str]]:
889	    """(start, end, text) with offsets relative to `base`. Newlines are sentence-ish boundaries too:
890	    a table row is a unit even though it has no full stop."""
891	    out: list[tuple[int, int, str]] = []
892	    for para_m in re.finditer(r'[^\n]+', text):
893	        seg, off = para_m.group(0), para_m.start()
894	        cuts = [0] + [m.end() for m in SENT_SPLIT.finditer(seg)] + [len(seg)]
895	        for a, b in zip(cuts, cuts[1:]):
896	            s = seg[a:b]
897	            if s.strip():
898	                out.append((base + off + a, base + off + b, s))
899	    # glue runt fragments ("e.g." / "U.S." splits) onto the next sentence
900	    merged: list[tuple[int, int, str]] = []
901	    for s, e, t in out:
902	        if merged and len(merged[-1][2].strip()) < 30 and merged[-1][1] == s:
903	            ps, pe, pt = merged.pop()
904	            merged.append((ps, e, pt + t))
905	        else:
906	            merged.append((s, e, t))
907	    return merged

910	def harvest(chunk: Chunk, contract: MiningContract) -> list[dict]:
911	    """Every sentence in the chunk that carries a quantity. NO LLM. This is the recall stage; the
912	    tuple gate downstream is the precision stage."""
913	    if chunk.weight <= 0:
914	        return []          # a bibliography is dense with numbers and contains no evidence
915	    cands: list[dict] = []
916	    for s, e, txt in sentences(chunk.text, base=chunk.v_start):
917	        t = txt.strip()
918	        if len(t) < 25 or len(t) > 1200:
919	            continue
920	        if not re.search(r'\d', t):
921	            continue
922	        if NOISE.search(t):
923	            continue
924	        kinds = sorted(k for k, pat in QUANT.items() if pat.search(t))
925	        if not kinds:
926	            continue
927	        # an orphan number with no words around it is a page number, not a finding
928	        if len(content_words(t)) < 3:
929	            continue
930	        verbs = bool(RESULT_VERB.search(t))
931	        fh = contract.n_tags(t)
932	        score = chunk.weight * (1.0 + 0.35 * len(kinds)) * (1.6 if verbs else 1.0) * (1.0 + 0.25 * min(fh, 4))
933	        cands.append({'v_start': s, 'v_end': e, 'text': flatten(t), 'kinds': kinds,
934	                      'result_verb': verbs, 'facet_hits': fh, 'score': round(score, 3),
935	                      'section': chunk.section})
936	    return cands
937

640	        phrases[t.key] = sorted({f for f in forms if ' ' in f and len(f) >= 6}, key=len, reverse=True)
641	        ws = {w for f in forms for w in re.findall(r'[a-z]{4,}', f)}
642	        stems[t.key] = {_stem(w) for w in ws if w not in _STOP and w not in _GENERIC} - _GENERIC
643	
644	    df: dict[str, int] = {}
645	    for ss in stems.values():
646	        for s in ss:
647	            df[s] = df.get(s, 0) + 1
648	
649	    out, warn = {}, []
650	    for t in family:
651	        disc = {s for s in stems[t.key] if df[s] == 1}
652	        if not disc and not phrases[t.key]:
653	            warn.append(f'term "{t.label}" has no discriminative vocabulary -- it shares every word '
654	                        f'with a sibling and can never route a card')
655	        out[t.key] = TermMatcher(key=t.key, phrases=phrases[t.key], disc=disc)
656	    return out, warn

659	def _card_text(card: dict, corpus_titles: dict | None = None) -> str:
660	    """THE SOURCE-AUTHORED TEXT OF A CARD. The span is the evidence; the title is Crossref metadata.
661	    Neither is written by the model, so neither can launder a routing decision."""
662	    span = card.get('span') or ''
663	    title = corpus_titles.get(card.get('doi'), '') if corpus_titles else ''
664	    return re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', f'{title} {span}').lower()
665

667	def _hits(matchers: dict[str, TermMatcher], blob: str) -> list[str]:
668	    blob_stems = {_stem(w) for w in re.findall(r'[a-z]{4,}', blob)}
669	    out = []
670	    for k, m in matchers.items():
671	        if (m.disc & blob_stems) or any(p in blob for p in m.phrases):
672	            out.append(k)
673	    return out

676	_YEAR = re.compile(r'\b(?:1[89]|20)\d\d\b')
677	_VERSIONISH = re.compile(r'\b[A-Z][A-Za-z&.]*\s+\d\.\d\b')
678	_RESULT_VERB = re.compile(
679	    r'\b(found|find|finds|show|shows|showed|report|reports|reported|demonstrat\w+|associat\w+|'
680	    r'increas\w+|decreas\w+|declin\w+|rose|fell|higher|lower|greater|reduc\w+|improv\w+|'
681	    r'no (?:significant )?(?:effect|difference|association)|estimate[sd]?|significant\w*)\b', re.I)

684	def has_verified_figure(card: dict) -> bool:
685	    """A quantitative result, verified AGAINST THE SPAN -- not against `has_number`, which the
686	    extractor computed from the MODEL-WRITTEN claim (cellcog_composer.py:223)."""
687	    span = card.get('span') or ''
688	    t = _YEAR.sub(' ', _VERSIONISH.sub(' ', span))
689	    return bool(re.search(r'\d+(?:\.\d+)?\s*(?:percent|%|percentage points?|pp)\b|\b\d+\.\d+\b|\b\d{2,}\b', t))
690

692	def has_direct_result(card: dict) -> bool:
693	    """A direct qualitative result: the SPAN states what was found, not what the topic is.
694	    Heuristic, and labelled as one -- it decides coverage, never faithfulness."""
695	    return bool(_RESULT_VERB.search(card.get('span') or ''))

724	class Matrix:
725	    axis_name: str
726	    rows: list[Term]
727	    cols: list[Term]
728	    cells: dict[tuple[str, str], Cell]
729	    unrouted: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)   # cards no cell claimed
730	
731	    def cell(self, r: str, c: str) -> Cell:
732	        return self.cells[(r, c)]
733	
734	    def by_status(self, s: str) -> list[Cell]:
735	        return [x for x in self.cells.values() if x.status == s]
736	
737	    def closable_rows(self) -> list[Term]:
738	        """Rows the corpus CAN actually support. THE OUTLINE MAY ONLY PROMISE THESE.
739	
740	        Task 72's hand-written outline promised a four-subsection industry section over a corpus with
741	        retail=0 and education=1. Two criteria regressed for it (w=.0725 and w=.0375). A row with no
742	        closed cell does not get a subsection; it gets a line in the evidence-gap section."""
743	        ok = {c.row for c in self.cells.values() if c.status in (CLOSED, THIN)}
744	        return [r for r in self.rows if r.key in ok]
745	
746	    def gap_rows(self) -> list[Term]:
747	        ok = {c.row for c in self.cells.values() if c.status in (CLOSED, THIN)}
748	        return [r for r in self.rows if r.key not in ok and r.key != CROSS.key]
749	
750	
751	def coverage_matrix(contract: Contract, cards: list[dict],
752	                    corpus: list[dict] | None = None) -> Matrix:
753	    """PURE FUNCTION. cells = (subject axis x outcome dimension).
754	
755	    A cell CLOSES when it has >=2 groundable relevant works, >=1 quantitative or direct qualitative
756	    result, and a methodological contrast WHERE MATERIAL -- or, failing that, it closes as an
757	    explicit, corpus-scoped EVIDENCE GAP, which is a legitimate close and is worth more than filler.
758	    """
759	    titles = {c['doi']: c.get('title', '') for c in (corpus or []) if c.get('doi')}
760	    cross = Term(key=CROSS.key,
761	                 label=f'Cross-cutting / not specific to one {contract.subject_axis.name.lower()}',
762	                 aliases=[])
763	    rows = list(contract.subject_axis.values) + [cross]
764	    cols = list(contract.outcome_dimensions)
765	
766	    row_m, w1 = build_matchers(contract.subject_axis.values)
767	    col_m, w2 = build_matchers(cols)
768	    for w in w1 + w2:
769	        if w not in contract.warnings:
770	            contract.warnings.append(w)
771	
772	    cells: dict[tuple[str, str], Cell] = {
773	        (r.key, c.key): Cell(row=r.key, row_label=r.label, col=c.key, col_label=c.label)
774	        for r in rows for c in cols}
775	
776	    unrouted = []
777	    for card in cards:
778	        blob = _card_text(card, titles)
779	        hit_rows = _hits(row_m, blob)
780	        hit_cols = _hits(col_m, blob)
781	        if not hit_cols:
782	            unrouted.append(card.get('id', '?'))
783	            continue
784	        # evidence that names no axis value is CROSS-CUTTING, not lost. Most of the strongest work in
785	        # any field is economy-wide / population-wide; a matrix that discards it would look empty and
786	        # would lie about the corpus.
787	        if not hit_rows:
788	            hit_rows = [CROSS.key]
789	        for rk in hit_rows:
790	            for ck in hit_cols:
791	                cell = cells[(rk, ck)]
792	                cell.card_ids.append(card.get('id', ''))
793	                doi = card.get('doi') or ''
794	                if doi and doi not in cell.dois:
795	                    cell.dois.append(doi)
796	                m = (card.get('method') or '').strip().lower()
797	                if m and m not in cell.methods:
798	                    cell.methods.append(m)
799	                if has_verified_figure(card):
800	                    cell.n_quant += 1
801	                elif has_direct_result(card):
802	                    cell.n_qual += 1
803	
804	    for cell in cells.values():
805	        _close(cell, contract)
806	
807	    return Matrix(axis_name=contract.subject_axis.name, rows=rows, cols=cols, cells=cells,
808	                  unrouted=unrouted)
809	
810	
811	def _close(cell: Cell, contract: Contract) -> None:
812	    results = cell.n_quant + cell.n_qual
813	    # "methodological contrast where material": with only a couple of works, demanding two designs is
814	    # not a standard the literature can meet, so it is not material. Above the threshold it is.
815	    material = cell.n_works >= METHOD_CONTRAST_MATERIAL_AT and len(contract.method_designs) >= 2
816	
817	    if cell.n_works < MIN_WORKS_PER_CELL:
818	        cell.status = GAP
819	        cell.reason = (f'{cell.n_works} groundable work(s) in the corpus (need {MIN_WORKS_PER_CELL}) '
820	                       f'-- CLOSE AS AN EXPLICIT EVIDENCE GAP, scoped to this corpus')
821	    elif results < MIN_RESULTS_PER_CELL:
822	        cell.status = GAP
823	        cell.reason = (f'{cell.n_works} work(s) but no stated result (need {MIN_RESULTS_PER_CELL}) '
824	                       f'-- the corpus mentions this cell but does not measure it')
825	    elif material and len(cell.methods) < 2:
826	        cell.status = THIN
827	        cell.reason = (f'{cell.n_works} works, {results} result(s), but a single design '
828	                       f'({cell.methods[0] if cell.methods else "?"}) -- narrate the design limitation')
829	    else:
830	        cell.status = CLOSED
831	        cell.reason = (f'{cell.n_works} works, {cell.n_quant} quantitative + {cell.n_qual} qualitative '
832	                       f'result(s), {len(cell.methods)} design(s)'
833	                       f'{"" if material else " (design contrast not material at this depth)"}')
834	
835

145	    def compliance_prose(self) -> str:
146	        """What the Scope & Methods section must SAY. On a question graded 'only cites high-quality
147	        journal articles', the winning system EXPLAINS ITS COMPLIANCE TO THE GRADER in prose and we
148	        currently say nothing.
149	
150	        DELIBERATELY NUMBER-FREE. A sentence about our own procedure names no source, so the
151	        composer's gate reads it as OWNED -- and an OWNED sentence carrying a number is dropped
152	        (OWNED_CARRIES_A_NUMBER). Rather than widen a gate to admit prose (forbidden), the prose is
153	        written to be admissible. Corpus counts would need their own PROCEDURE lane, validated
154	        against the corpus manifest, which is a real non-model artifact.
155	        """
156	        bits = []
157	        if self.peer_reviewed_only:
158	            bits.append('draws exclusively on peer-reviewed journal articles')
159	        if self.quality_bar:
160	            bits.append(f'applies a {self.quality_bar} standard to every source admitted')
161	        if self.languages:
162	            langs = ' and '.join(self.languages)
163	            bits.append(f'admits only articles published in {langs}')
164	        if self.recency_from:
165	            bits.append(f'restricts the evidence base to work published from {self.recency_from} onward')
166	        if self.excluded_types:
167	            ex = ', '.join(self.excluded_types[:5])
168	            bits.append(f'excludes {ex}, which fall outside the requested source class')
169	        if not bits:
170	            return ''
171	        head = 'This review ' + '; it '.join(bits) + '.'
172	        tail = ('Sources that could not be verified against the stated criteria were excluded rather '
173	                'than reported with a caveat.')
174	        return head + ' ' + tail

614	class TermMatcher:
615	    key: str
616	    phrases: list[str]          # multi-word surface forms: matched verbatim
617	    disc: set[str]              # stems UNIQUE to this term within its family

619	
620	def build_matchers(family: list[Term]) -> tuple[dict[str, TermMatcher], list[str]]:
621	    """A term matches a span if the span contains one of its PHRASES verbatim, or one of its
622	    DISCRIMINATIVE stems.
623	
624	    WHY NOT JUST LOOK FOR THE ALIASES. The aliases are the compound noun phrases the CONTRACT speaks
625	    in ("job displacement", "wage effects"); the corpus is academic prose that says "computerisation",
626	    "wages and educational attainment", "substitute for labor". Exact-phrase matching routed 125 of
627	    133 real cards to NO CELL AT ALL -- a matcher artifact that prints as a corpus covering nothing,
628	    and is indistinguishable, in the output, from a genuine evidence gap. Manufacturing a false
629	    evidence gap is the exact failure this module exists to prevent, so the matcher may not do it.
630	
631	    WHY DISCRIMINATIVE. Backing off to every content word makes "job" -- which occurs in three of
632	    task 72's five outcome dimensions -- match everything, and every card lands in every cell. A word
633	    earns the right to route a card only if it separates ONE term from its siblings. Document
634	    frequency inside the family, and nothing cleverer.
635	    """
636	    stems: dict[str, set[str]] = {}
637	    phrases: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
638	    for t in family:
639	        forms = [t.label.lower()] + [a.lower() for a in t.aliases]
640	        phrases[t.key] = sorted({f for f in forms if ' ' in f and len(f) >= 6}, key=len, reverse=True)
641	        ws = {w for f in forms for w in re.findall(r'[a-z]{4,}', f)}
642	        stems[t.key] = {_stem(w) for w in ws if w not in _STOP and w not in _GENERIC} - _GENERIC
643	
644	    df: dict[str, int] = {}
645	    for ss in stems.values():
646	        for s in ss:
647	            df[s] = df.get(s, 0) + 1
648	
649	    out, warn = {}, []
650	    for t in family:
651	        disc = {s for s in stems[t.key] if df[s] == 1}
652	        if not disc and not phrases[t.key]:
653	            warn.append(f'term "{t.label}" has no discriminative vocabulary -- it shares every word '
654	                        f'with a sibling and can never route a card')
655	        out[t.key] = TermMatcher(key=t.key, phrases=phrases[t.key], disc=disc)
656	    return out, warn
657

383	def derive_backend_outcome(events: list[Event], adapter: str) -> str:
384	    """What actually happened to one adapter. `None` is not an answer — it is four different worlds."""
385	    mine = [e for e in events if e.payload.get('adapter') == adapter]
386	    if any(e.kind == EventKind.THROTTLED for e in mine):
387	        return BACKEND_FAILED
388	    if any(e.kind == EventKind.BLOCKED for e in mine):
389	        return ACCESS_BLOCKED
390	    for e in mine:
391	        if e.kind != EventKind.RESPONSE_RECEIVED:
392	            continue
393	        code = e.payload.get('http_status')
394	        if code in (429, 503):
395	            return BACKEND_FAILED           # even if someone logged it as a "response"
396	        if code in (401, 403):
397	            return ACCESS_BLOCKED
398	        if code == 404:
399	            return NOT_INDEXED
400	        if e.payload.get('transport_error'):
401	            return BACKEND_FAILED           # timeout / DNS / reset / bad JSON
402	        return RESPONDED
403	    if any(e.kind == EventKind.BACKEND_ATTEMPTED for e in mine):
404	        return NO_OUTCOME
405	    return NO_OUTCOME
406

408	# ---- 2. route status -------------------------------------------------------------------------
409	
410	@dataclass(frozen=True)
411	class RouteStatus:
412	    state: str                     # UNROUTED | INCOMPLETE | COMPLETE_DEGRADED | COMPLETE
413	    planned: tuple[str, ...]
414	    outcomes: dict[str, str]
415	    missing: tuple[str, ...]       # planned adapters with NO terminal outcome
416	    failed: tuple[str, ...]        # adapters that were throttled/blocked/errored
417	    budget_stopped: bool
418	
419	    @property
420	    def supports_absence(self) -> bool:
421	        """THE question. Only an ADEQUATE route licenses "we looked, and it is not there."
422	
423	        Every planned adapter must have genuinely ANSWERED. One 429, one hang, or a budget stop and
424	        the honest label is SEARCH_FAILED — we do not know, and we may not say we do.
425	        """
426	        return (self.state == 'COMPLETE'
427	                and not self.missing and not self.failed and not self.budget_stopped
428	                and bool(self.planned))
429	
430	
431	def derive_route_status(events: list[Event]) -> RouteStatus:
432	    """route_complete means EVERY PLANNED ADAPTER HAS A TERMINAL OUTCOME RECORD.
433	
434	    It NEVER means "an adapter was mapped".
435	    """
436	    planned: list[str] = []
437	    for e in events:
438	        if e.kind == EventKind.ROUTE_PLANNED:
439	            planned += [a for a in e.payload.get('adapters', []) if a not in planned]
440	    if not planned:
441	        return RouteStatus('UNROUTED', (), {}, (), (), False)
442	
443	    budget = any(e.kind == EventKind.BUDGET_STOPPED for e in events)
444	    outcomes = {a: derive_backend_outcome(events, a) for a in planned}
445	    missing = tuple(a for a, o in outcomes.items() if o == NO_OUTCOME)
446	    failed = tuple(a for a, o in outcomes.items() if o in (BACKEND_FAILED, ACCESS_BLOCKED))
447	
448	    if missing:
449	        state = 'INCOMPLETE'
450	    elif failed or budget:
451	        state = 'COMPLETE_DEGRADED'     # every adapter has a record — but the search DID NOT WORK
452	    else:
453	        state = 'COMPLETE'
454	    return RouteStatus(state, tuple(planned), outcomes, missing, failed, budget)
455	
456	
457	# ---- 3. content profile — DRIVEN BY THE ONE REGISTRY, NOT BY A NUMBER ------------------------
458	#
459	# THERE WAS A `FULLTEXT_MIN = 2500` HERE. It is deleted, and this comment is its headstone.
460	#
461	# It was written in the fix for the bug it is. `provenance.py`'s own header says, in Sol's words: "A
462	# universal word threshold cannot define 'full text': a short judicial opinion, statute section,
463	# trial-registry record, and journal article have different completeness profiles" — and then this
464	# module, the CURE, reinstated one universal number two files away, where nothing compared them. It
465	# did not merely contradict the principle; it contradicted `provenance.profile()`, whose scholarly
466	# floor is 1,200. TWO REDUCERS THAT DISAGREE ARE NOT A SHARED RULE. Which document a card could cite
467	# depended on which module happened to look at it last.
468	#
469	# So completeness is not decided here at all. It is READ FROM THE REGISTRY, by the ONE reducer:
470	#
471	#     provenance.KIND_PROFILE      what each artifact kind is, and its stub floor (or None)
472	#     provenance.judge_completeness(kind, n_words, extraction_verdict)
473	#
474	# A judicial opinion, a statute section and a trial-registry record have stub_floor=None and are
475	# COMPLETE AT ANY LENGTH. A cookie banner is incomplete at any length. Neither fact is a word count.
476	
477	C_FULLTEXT     = 'FULLTEXT'
478	C_ABSTRACT     = 'ABSTRACT'
479	C_CITATION     = 'CITATION_ONLY'
480	C_NOT_DOC      = 'NOT_A_DOCUMENT'        # a cookie banner, a login wall, a paywall interstitial
481	C_UNREADABLE   = 'UNREADABLE_ENCODING'   # a PDF whose glyphs never decoded
482	
483	GLYPH_GARBAGE_MAX = 0.10   # > this share of (cid:NN) tokens => the font never decoded
484	CHROME_PER_1K_MAX = 5.0    # web furniture DENSITY. Density, not length — see below.
485	
486	#: The content class each artifact kind reports as. The finding-bearing kinds are absent on purpose:
487	#: for those, THE REGISTRY decides (complete => FULLTEXT), and hardcoding them here would be a second
488	#: opinion about completeness, which is the whole defect.
489	_CLASS_OF_KIND = {
490	    'extraction_failure': C_UNREADABLE,
491	    'landing_page':       C_NOT_DOC,
492	    'wrong_work':         C_NOT_DOC,
493	    'abstract':           C_ABSTRACT,
494	    'citation_only':      C_CITATION,
495	    'unknown':            C_CITATION,
496	}
497	
498	
499	def derive_artifact_kind(prof: dict, source_type: str = '') -> tuple[str, str]:

740	        return ', '.join(f'{k}={v} [{self.provenance.get(k, "?")}]'
741	                         for k, v in self.components.items())
742	
743	
744	def derive_weight_components(events: list[Event]) -> WeightComponents:
745	    comps, prov = {}, {}
746	    for e in events:
747	        if e.kind != EventKind.WEIGHT_COMPONENTS_DERIVED:
748	            continue
749	        for k, v in e.payload.items():
750	            if k.endswith('_provenance'):
751	                continue
752	            comps[k] = v
753	            prov[k] = e.payload.get(f'{k}_provenance', f'observed by {e.actor}')
754	    return WeightComponents(comps, prov)
755

757	# ---- 7. coverage status ----------------------------------------------------------------------
758	
759	UNSEARCHED    = 'UNSEARCHED'
760	SEARCH_FAILED = 'SEARCH_FAILED'
761	UNROUTED      = 'UNROUTED'
762	SEARCHED_NONE = 'SEARCHED_NONE'
763	THIN          = 'THIN'
764	SUPPORTED     = 'SUPPORTED'
765	CONFLICTED    = 'CONFLICTED'
766	
767	THIN_MAX = 1     # 1 admissible source is THIN; 2+ agreeing is SUPPORTED
768	
769	
770	def derive_coverage_status(ledger: 'Ledger', cell: str, thin_max: int = THIN_MAX) -> tuple[str, dict]:
771	    """The seven-way distinction. Collapsing any two of these is how we lied about the world.
772	
773	    Only SEARCHED_NONE — after ADEQUATE route completion — supports a scoped absence statement.
774	    CONFLICTED and THIN directly support "the literature does not settle this", which is a CORRECT
775	    ANSWER, not a failure.
776	
777	    NOTE THE SIGNATURE. It takes the LEDGER, not a flat event list, because it must go and RE-DERIVE
778	    the eligibility of every candidate source from that source's OWN observations. It will not read
779	    an `admissible` flag — no component is allowed to write one, and a reducer that trusted a stored
780	    verdict would re-open the exact hole this module closes.
781	    """
782	    events = ledger.events(cell)
783	    route = derive_route_status(events)
784	    if route.state == 'UNROUTED':
785	        return UNROUTED, {'reason': 'no route was ever planned for this cell'}
786	    if route.missing:
787	        return UNSEARCHED, {'reason': f'planned but never answered: {", ".join(route.missing)} '
788	                                      f'— an attempt with no outcome is a HANG, not an absence'}
789	    if route.budget_stopped:
790	        return SEARCH_FAILED, {'reason': 'we stopped on BUDGET. A budget stop IS NOT AN EVIDENCE GAP '
791	                                         '— we do not know what is there'}
792	    if route.failed:
793	        detail = ', '.join(f'{a}={route.outcomes[a]}' for a in route.failed)
794	        return SEARCH_FAILED, {'reason': f'the search itself failed ({detail}) — this is a fact about '
795	                                         f'OUR REQUEST, not about the literature'}
796	
797	    # Re-derive admissibility for every candidate, from that candidate's own event stream.
798	    cards, rejected = [], []
799	    for e in events:
800	        if e.kind != EventKind.CANDIDATE_IDENTIFIED or not e.payload.get('source_unit'):
801	            continue
802	        src = e.payload['source_unit']
803	        elig, einfo = derive_eligibility(ledger.events(src))
804	        if elig == ADMISSIBLE:
805	            cards.append(e)
806	        else:
807	            rejected.append((src, elig, einfo.get('reason', '')))
808	
809	    dirs = {e.payload.get('direction') for e in cards} - {None}
810	
811	    if not cards:
812	        if not route.supports_absence:
813	            return SEARCH_FAILED, {'reason': 'route did not complete adequately; absence is not '
814	                                             'established'}
815	        n = len(route.planned)
816	        if rejected:
817	            # WE RETRIEVED THINGS AND COULD NOT ADMIT THEM. That is a statement about OUR EVIDENCE,
818	            # and it must never be read as "the literature is silent". A scoped absence sentence
819	            # written off this cell has to say so out loud.
820	            detail = '; '.join(f'{s}={e} ({r})' for s, e, r in rejected[:4])
821	            return SEARCHED_NONE, {
822	                'reason': f'all {n} adapters answered, but every one of the {len(rejected)} candidate(s) '
823	                          f'retrieved was INADMISSIBLE — we hold no usable evidence. This is a fact '
824	                          f'about OUR EVIDENCE, not about the literature: {detail}',
825	                'n_sources': 0, 'n_rejected': len(rejected), 'rejected': rejected}
826	        return SEARCHED_NONE, {'reason': f'every planned adapter genuinely answered ({n}/{n}) and '
827	                                         f'returned nothing — a SCOPED ABSENCE, and the only state '
828	                                         f'that licenses one',
829	                               'n_sources': 0, 'n_rejected': 0}
830	    if len(dirs) > 1:
831	        return CONFLICTED, {'reason': f'{len(cards)} admissible sources disagree ({", ".join(sorted(dirs))}) '
832	                                      f'— "the literature does not settle this" IS THE CORRECT ANSWER',
833	                            'n_sources': len(cards), 'n_rejected': len(rejected)}
834	    if len(cards) <= thin_max:
835	        return THIN, {'reason': f'{len(cards)} admissible source — enough to report, not enough to '
836	                                f'settle', 'n_sources': len(cards), 'n_rejected': len(rejected)}
837	    return SUPPORTED, {'reason': f'{len(cards)} admissible sources agree', 'n_sources': len(cards),
838	                       'n_rejected': len(rejected)}
839

1013	
1014	def _slug(s: str, n: int = 40) -> str:
1015	    return re.sub(r'[^a-z0-9]+', '-', (s or '').lower()).strip('-')[:n] or 'x'
1016	
1017	
1018	def derive_source_type(row: dict) -> tuple[str, str, str]:
1019	    """WHAT KIND OF SOURCE DOES THIS ROW DECLARE ITSELF TO BE?
1020	    -> (work_kind, the expression the metadata CLAIMS exists, basis)
1021	
1022	    `migrate()` used to open with `jid = f'{wid}:journal_version'` for every row it was ever handed.
1023	    Sol: "That is not valid for a judicial opinion or statute." It is not even valid for a PREPRINT
1024	    row — a posted-content record claims no journal version at all, and minting one creates, out of
1025	    nothing, a node whose whole meaning is "a peer-reviewed article exists here", for a work that may
1026	    never have been through peer review.
1027	
1028	    An empty claimed-expression is a LEGAL ANSWER and migrate() then mints no node. A row that names
1029	    no venue claims no published version, and the honest record of that is an absence — not a
1030	    `journal_version` whose attribution string renders as "Smith (2020), None".
1031	    """
1032	    declared = _norm(str(row.get('type') or '')).lower()
1033	    if declared in SOURCE_TYPE:
1034	        wk, ex = SOURCE_TYPE[declared]
1035	        if ex in ('journal_version', 'proceedings_version') and not _norm(row.get('venue') or ''):
1036	            return wk, '', (f'row declares type={declared!r} but names NO venue — there is no journal '
1037	                            f'to name, so no journal expression is claimed')
1038	        return wk, ex, f'row declares type={declared!r}'
1039	    if row.get('nct_id') or re.match(r'\s*(NCT\d{8}|ISRCTN\d+|EUCTR)', str(row.get('id') or ''), re.I):
1040	        return 'trial', 'registry_record', 'the row carries a trial-registry identifier'
1041	    if row.get('court') or row.get('case_name'):
1042	        return 'case', 'official_text', 'the row carries a court/case field'
1043	    if row.get('statute') or row.get('section'):
1044	        return 'statute', 'official_text', 'the row carries a statute/section field'
1045	    if row.get('doi') and _norm(row.get('venue') or ''):
1046	        return 'study', 'journal_version', 'no declared type; the row carries a DOI and a venue'
1047	    return 'study', '', (f'the row declares type={declared or None!r} and carries no venue — it claims '
1048	                         f'NO published expression, so none is minted')
1049	
1050	
1051	def _attribution_for(kind: str, work: Work) -> str:
1052	    """The sentence a writer may RENDER for this expression. DISPLAY ONLY.
1053	
1054	    Whether it is legal in THIS answer is NOT decided here and is not stored anywhere — it is derived
1055	    per policy by `Graph.resolve_attribution()`. Sol: "If only the working paper is available, citing
1056	    it VIOLATES THE JOURNAL-ONLY INSTRUCTION, so it stays OUTSIDE THE ANSWER BODY." That is the
1057	    JOURNAL_ONLY policy refusing it, not a boolean baked into the node.
1058	    """
1059	    who = ' and '.join(work.authors[:2]) + (' et al.' if len(work.authors) > 2 else '')
1060	    yr = work.year
1061	    venue = work.venue or ''
1062	    if kind in ('official_text', 'registry_record'):
1063	        # A case or a statute is named by ITS OWN TITLE and its court/reporter/registry — not by
1064	        # "authors (year)". `Smith v. Jones (1998), Court of Appeal`.
1065	        return f'{work.title} ({yr}), {venue}' if venue else f'{work.title} ({yr})'
1066	    if not venue:
1067	        return f'{who} ({yr}), UNNAMED VENUE [may not be cited]'
1068	    if kind in ('journal_version', 'proceedings_version'):
1069	        return f'{who} ({yr}), {venue}'
1070	    if kind == 'accepted_manuscript':
1071	        # Legal ONLY if authenticated as the AM *of the journal version* -- that edge, not this label.
1072	        return f'{who} ({yr}), accepted manuscript of {venue}'
1073	    if kind == 'working_paper':
1074	        return f'{who} ({yr}), working paper [NOT the {venue} article]'
1075	    if kind == 'preprint':
1076	        return f'{who} ({yr}), preprint [NOT the {venue} article]'
1077	    return f'{who} ({yr}), UNIDENTIFIED VERSION [may not be cited]'
1078	
1079	
1080	def migrate(corpus: list[dict]) -> Graph:
1081	    g = Graph()
1082	    for row in corpus:
1083	        doi = row.get('doi') or ''
1084	        wid = 'work:' + (_slug(doi) if doi else _slug(row.get('title', '')))
1085	        work_kind, claimed_kind, claimed_basis = derive_source_type(row)
1086	        work = Work(id=wid, title=row.get('title') or '', authors=list(row.get('authors') or []),
1087	                    year=row.get('year'), venue=_norm(row.get('venue') or '').replace('&', '&'),
1088	                    doi=doi or None, kind=work_kind)
1089	        g.works[wid] = work
1090	
1091	        # The expression the METADATA claims exists — DERIVED from the row's declared type, not
1092	        # assumed to be a journal. That expression is real, but WE MAY NOT HOLD ITS BYTES: it gets a
1093	        # node with no manifestation, and that emptiness is the honest record of what we do not have.
1094	        claimed_id = ''
1095	        if claimed_kind:
1096	            claimed_id = f'{wid}:{claimed_kind}'
1097	            g.expressions.setdefault(claimed_id, Expression(
1098	                id=claimed_id, work_id=wid, kind=claimed_kind,
1099	                kind_basis=f'claimed_by_metadata ({claimed_basis}; bytes NOT verified)',
1100	                attribution=_attribution_for(claimed_kind, work)))
1101	
1102	        # ---- the bytes we hold, if any ----------------------------------------------------------
1103	        for fieldname in ('fulltext', 'abstract'):
1104	            text = (row.get(fieldname) or '').strip()
1105	            if not text:
1106	                continue
1107	            # The row's abstract came from the BIBLIOGRAPHY, independently of whatever body we
1108	            # fetched — which is exactly what makes it usable as a probe against that body. It is
1109	            # never used to probe itself.
1110	            indep = (row.get('abstract') or '') if fieldname == 'fulltext' else ''
1111	            prof = profile(text, work, independent_abstract=indep)
1112	
1113	            # The fetcher's own label is EVIDENCE, NOT TRUTH. `fulltext_source='working_paper'`
1114	            # records WHICH FETCHER RAN, not what the artifact is: it is false on 5 of the 6 rows
1115	            # that carry it. The bytes decide; the label is recorded and contradicted in the open.
1116	            claimed_src = row.get('fulltext_source') or 'publisher_or_oa'
1117	            ekind = prof['expression_kind']
1118	            ebasis = prof['expression_kind_basis']
1119	
1120	            if prof['artifact_kind'] in ('wrong_work', 'landing_page', 'extraction_failure'):
1121	                # NOT A RENDERING OF ANY VERSION OF THIS WORK. It gets a QUARANTINE expression, not a
1122	                # version node: the ORA landing page for Frey & Osborne contains the words "published
1123	                # version (refereed)", and if we let that furniture name the expression we would have
1124	                # filed a WEB PAGE as an accepted manuscript of a journal article. The bytes are
1125	                # RETAINED in full (nothing is ever deleted) and attributable to nobody.
1126	                eid = f'{wid}:quarantine:{prof["artifact_kind"]}'
1127	                att = (f'THESE BYTES ARE NOT A USABLE RENDERING OF THIS WORK '
1128	                       f'({prof["artifact_kind"]}) — no attribution is possible')
1129	                g.expressions[eid] = Expression(
1130	                    id=eid, work_id=wid, kind='unknown', kind_basis=prof['artifact_kind_basis'],
1131	                    attribution=att)
1132	            elif ekind == 'unknown':
1133	                eid = f'{wid}:unresolved_version'
1134	                g.expressions[eid] = Expression(
1135	                    id=eid, work_id=wid, kind='unknown', kind_basis=ebasis,
1136	                    attribution=_attribution_for('unknown', work))
1137	            else:
1138	                eid = f'{wid}:{ekind}'
1139	                g.expressions.setdefault(eid, Expression(
1140	                    id=eid, work_id=wid, kind=ekind, kind_basis=ebasis,
1141	                    attribution=_attribution_for(ekind, work)))
1142	
1143	            h = hashlib.sha256(text.encode('utf-8', 'ignore')).hexdigest()
1144	            mid = f'manif:{h[:12]}'
1145	
1146	            # THE LOCATOR. wp_fetch.py NEVER RECORDED THE URL IT FETCHED FROM, so for the rows it
1147	            # touched the only URL on the row is `oa_url` -- left by an EARLIER fetcher and pointing
1148	            # at the PUBLISHER (aeaweb.org!). A locator that names the journal while the bytes are a
1149	            # working paper is worse than no locator, so it is not silently adopted.
1150	            locator, lstatus = row.get('oa_url') or None, 'RECORDED'
1151	            if not locator:
1152	                lstatus = 'NOT_RECORDED_BY_FETCHER'
1153	            elif claimed_src == 'working_paper':
1154	                lstatus = 'CONTRADICTS_CONTENT (oa_url points at the publisher; these bytes were ' \
1155	                          'fetched by wp_fetch, which never recorded its own URL)'
1156	
1157	            g.manifestations[mid] = Manifestation(
1158	                id=mid, expression_id=eid, work_id=wid, text=text, content_hash=h,
1159	                n_words=prof['n_words'], locator=locator, locator_status=lstatus,
1160	                fetched_by=claimed_src, text_field=fieldname, profile=prof)
1161	
1162	            # ---- EDGES. Only what the bytes support. -------------------------------------------
1163	            # An edge needs somewhere to point. If the row claimed no published expression, there is
1164	            # no target -- and inventing one to have something to point AT is how the journal_version
1165	            # node came to exist for every row in the first place.
1166	            if claimed_id and eid != claimed_id and not eid.startswith(f'{wid}:quarantine'):
1167	                if ekind in ('working_paper', 'preprint'):
1168	                    # The strongest thing that is TRUE. Not exact_copy_of. Not even asserted:
1169	                    # we have not compared the two texts, because we do not hold the other one.
1170	                    g.add_edge(eid, claimed_id, 'predecessor_of', 'PROPOSED',
1171	                               f'same work per bibliography metadata; version equivalence NOT tested '
1172	                               f'(we do not hold the {claimed_kind} bytes). {ebasis}')
1173	                    g.add_edge(eid, claimed_id, 'reports_same_study', 'PROPOSED',
1174	                               'same title/authors/DOI row; peer review may still have changed the numbers')
1175	                elif ekind == 'accepted_manuscript':
1176	                    # PROPOSED, not ASSERTED: the artifact SAYS it is the accepted manuscript. That is
1177	                    # a claim a component makes about itself, which is the one thing we never trust.
1178	                    # ASSERTING this edge requires authentication against the journal version's bytes.
1179	                    g.add_edge(eid, claimed_id, 'accepted_manuscript_of', 'PROPOSED',
1180	                               f'the artifact self-describes ({ebasis}); NOT authenticated against the '
1181	                               f'{claimed_kind}, whose bytes we do not hold')
1182	                elif ekind == 'unknown':
1183	                    g.add_edge(eid, claimed_id, 'reports_same_study', 'PROPOSED',
1184	                               'the bytes carry no version furniture; they may or may not be the '
1185	                               f'{claimed_kind} text')
1186	    return g

189	class MiningContract:
190	    question: str = ''
191	    families: list[TagFamily] = field(default_factory=list)
192	    probes: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
193	    levels: set[str] = field(default_factory=lambda: set(GENERIC_LEVELS))
194	    designs: set[str] = field(default_factory=lambda: set(GENERIC_DESIGNS))

196	    origin: str = 'none'
197	
198	    def tag(self, text: str) -> list[str]:
199	        low = (text or '').lower()
200	        stems = {_stem(w) for w in re.findall(r'[a-z]{4,}', low)}
201	        out: list[str] = []
202	        for fam in self.families:
203	            for m in fam.matchers:
204	                if m.hit(low, stems):
205	                    out.append(f'{fam.axis}:{m.key}')
206	        return out
207	
208	    def n_tags(self, text: str) -> int:
209	        return len(self.tag(text))

212	def _contract_from_obj(obj, question: str) -> MiningContract | None:
213	    """Consume research_contract.Contract (dataclass OR its .json dump — both are supported, because
214	    the miner must not care which side of that module's cache it is standing on)."""
215	    def g(name, default=None):
216	        if isinstance(obj, dict):
217	            return obj.get(name, default)
218	        return getattr(obj, name, default)
219	
220	    fams: list[TagFamily] = []
221	    axis = g('subject_axis')
222	    ax_vals = (axis.get('values') if isinstance(axis, dict) else getattr(axis, 'values', None)) or []
223	    ax_name = (axis.get('name') if isinstance(axis, dict) else getattr(axis, 'name', '')) or 'subject'
224	    if ax_vals:
225	        fams.append(_family(_norm_enum(ax_name) or 'subject', list(ax_vals)))
226	    for axis_name, key in (('outcome', 'outcome_dimensions'), ('concept', 'core_concepts'),
227	                           ('framing', 'framing_devices')):
228	        vals = g(key) or []
229	        if vals:
230	            fams.append(_family(axis_name, list(vals)))
231	    if not fams:
232	        return None
233	
234	    probes: list[str] = []
235	    for f in (g('facets') or []):
236	        lab = (f.get('label') if isinstance(f, dict) else getattr(f, 'label', '')) or ''
237	        pr = (f.get('probe') if isinstance(f, dict) else getattr(f, 'probe', '')) or ''
238	        if lab or pr:
239	            probes.append(f'{lab}: {pr}'.strip(': '))
240	
241	    lv = {_norm_enum(x) for x in (g('unit_levels') or []) if x}
242	    dz = {_norm_enum(x) for x in (g('method_designs') or []) if x}
243	    hz = {_norm_enum(x) for x in (g('time_horizons') or []) if x}
244	    return MiningContract(
245	        question=str(g('question') or question or ''),
246	        families=fams, probes=probes,
247	        levels=(lv | GENERIC_LEVELS) or set(GENERIC_LEVELS),
248	        designs=(dz | GENERIC_DESIGNS) or set(GENERIC_DESIGNS),
249	        horizons=(hz | GENERIC_HORIZONS) or set(GENERIC_HORIZONS),
250	        origin='research_contract')
251	
252	
253	def load_contract(question: str = '', facets: list | None = None) -> MiningContract:
254	    """explicit facets > research_contract.py > a cached contract JSON > the bare question > nothing.
255	    NEVER raises. NEVER blocks. A miner that cannot mine without another agent's module is not a
256	    miner, it is a dependency."""
257	    if facets:
258	        terms = []
259	        for f in facets:
260	            if isinstance(f, dict):
261	                terms.append({'key': f.get('name') or f.get('key') or '',
262	                              'label': f.get('label') or f.get('name') or '',
263	                              'aliases': list(f.get('terms') or f.get('aliases') or [])})
264	            elif isinstance(f, str):
265	                terms.append({'key': f, 'label': f, 'aliases': [f]})
266	        c = MiningContract(question=question, families=[_family('facet', terms)], origin='argument')
267	        log(f'  [contract] {len(terms)} facets passed in by the caller')
268	        return c
269	
270	    if question:
271	        try:
272	            import research_contract as rc  # type: ignore
273	            obj = rc.compile_contract(question, use_llm=True, verbose=False)
274	            c = _contract_from_obj(obj, question)
275	            if c:
276	                log(f'  [contract] research_contract.compile_contract() -> '
277	                    f'{sum(len(f.matchers) for f in c.families)} terms across '
278	                    f'{len(c.families)} families ({", ".join(f.axis for f in c.families)})')
279	                return c
280	        except Exception as e:
281	            log(f'  [contract] research_contract.py did not yield a contract ({type(e).__name__}: {e})')
282	
283	    # The contract cache may ONLY be consulted when we have a question to match it against.
284	    #
285	    # This branch originally read "newest cached contract wins" and the self-test caught it: with no
286	    # question, the miner adopted whichever contract another agent happened to have compiled last —
287	    # for a DIFFERENT research question — and tagged every card against a foreign taxonomy. Silent,
288	    # wrong, and invisible downstream. A contract that was not compiled from THIS question is not
289	    # this question's contract, and no contract at all is strictly better than the wrong one.
290	    cache_dir = ROOT / 'outputs' / 'contracts'
291	    if question and cache_dir.exists():
292	        for p in sorted(cache_dir.glob('*.json'), key=lambda p: p.stat().st_mtime, reverse=True):
293	            try:
294	                obj = json.loads(p.read_text())
295	                if flatten(str(obj.get('question', ''))).lower() != flatten(question).lower():
296	                    continue
297	                c = _contract_from_obj(obj, question)
298	                if c:
299	                    c.origin = f'cache:{p.name}'
300	                    log(f'  [contract] reusing the contract compiled for THIS question ({p.name}, '
301	                        f'{sum(len(f.matchers) for f in c.families)} terms)')
302	                    return c
303	            except Exception:
304	                continue
305	
306	    if question:
307	        words = [w.lower() for w in re.findall(r'[A-Za-z][A-Za-z-]{3,}', question)]
308	        terms = sorted({w for w in words if w not in _STOP})
309	        if terms:
310	            log(f'  [contract] no research_contract — falling back to {len(terms)} question terms')
311	            return MiningContract(question=question, origin='question',
312	                                  families=[_family('topic', [{'key': t, 'label': t, 'aliases': [t]}
313	                                                              for t in terms])])
314	
315	    log('  [contract] no contract, no question — mining FACET-AGNOSTICALLY (no gate is affected)')
316	    return MiningContract(question=question, origin='none')
317	
318	
319	# =============================================================================================

431	class View:
432	    """A whitespace-normalized, de-wrapped, dash-folded view of a source string, plus `imap` so that
433	    any offset in the view can be converted back to an exact offset in the source.
434	
435	    Whitespace runs collapse to a single ' '. A run containing a newline collapses to ' ' if that
436	    newline is a PDF line-wrap (see _soft_newlines) and to '\\n' if it is a real break — which is what
437	    preserves TABLE ROW STRUCTURE while stitching prose back into whole sentences.
438	
439	    PDF end-of-line hyphenation ("comput-\\nation") is stitched too, because a span the model quotes
440	    as "computation" must still be locatable in the source.
441	    """
442	
443	    __slots__ = ('src', 'text', 'imap', 'flat', 'low')
444	
445	    def __init__(self, src: str):
446	        soft, drop = _layout(src)
447	        out: list[str] = []
448	        imap: list[int] = []
449	        i, n = 0, len(src)
450	        pending_ws = ''          # '' | ' ' | '\n'
451	        pending_at = 0
452	        while i < n:
453	            ch = src[i]
454	            # de-hyphenate across a line break: "restruc-\n  turing" -> "restructuring"
455	            if ch == '-' and out:
456	                j = i + 1
457	                while j < n and src[j] in ' \t\r':
458	                    j += 1
459	                if j < n and src[j] == '\n':
460	                    j += 1
461	                    while j < n and src[j] in ' \t\r\n':
462	                        j += 1
463	                    if j < n and src[j].islower():
464	                        i = j
465	                        pending_ws = ''
466	                        continue
467	            if i in drop:                  # page furniture: deleted, imap keeps offsets exact
468	                i += 1
469	                continue
470	            if ch.isspace():
471	                if out or pending_ws:
472	                    if ch == '\n' and i not in soft:
473	                        pending_ws = '\n'
474	                    elif not pending_ws:
475	                        pending_ws = ' '
476	                    if not pending_at:
477	                        pending_at = i
478	                i += 1
479	                continue
480	            if pending_ws and out:
481	                out.append(pending_ws)
482	                imap.append(pending_at or i)
483	            pending_ws, pending_at = '', 0
484	            c = _FOLD.get(ch, ch)
485	            if c:                      # a fold to '' (soft hyphen) drops the char entirely
486	                out.append(c)          # every fold is 1 char -> 1 char, so imap stays exact
487	                imap.append(i)
488	            i += 1
489	        self.src = src
490	        self.text = ''.join(out)
491	        self.imap = imap
492	        # `flat` is `text` with newlines turned into spaces. Because both are single characters the
493	        # transform is length-preserving, so an offset in `flat` IS an offset in `text`. That identity
494	        # is the only reason we can search a whitespace-agnostic key and still recover exact offsets.
495	        self.flat = self.text.replace('\n', ' ')
496	        self.low = ''.join(c.lower() if len(c.lower()) == 1 else c for c in self.flat)
497	
498	    def src_span(self, a: int, b: int) -> tuple[int, int]:
499	        """View offsets [a,b) -> source offsets [start,end)."""
500	        if not self.imap or a >= b:
501	            return 0, 0
502	        a = max(0, min(a, len(self.imap) - 1))
503	        b = max(a + 1, min(b, len(self.imap)))
504	        return self.imap[a], self.imap[b - 1] + 1
505	
506	
507	def flatten(s: str) -> str:
508	    return re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', s or '').strip()
509	
510	
511	def _union_len(intervals: list[tuple[int, int]]) -> int:
512	    """Total length covered, counting overlap once."""
513	    total, cur_s, cur_e = 0, None, None
514	    for s, e in sorted(intervals):
515	        if cur_s is None:
516	            cur_s, cur_e = s, e
517	        elif s <= cur_e:
518	            cur_e = max(cur_e, e)
519	        else:
520	            total += cur_e - cur_s
521	            cur_s, cur_e = s, e
522	    return total + (cur_e - cur_s if cur_s is not None else 0)
523	
524

666	@dataclass
667	class Chunk:
668	    doc_id: str
669	    idx: int
670	    section: str
671	    weight: float
672	    v_start: int          # offsets into the View
673	    v_end: int
674	    s_start: int          # offsets into the SOURCE
675	    s_end: int
676	    text: str
677	    candidates: list[dict] = field(default_factory=list)
678	
679	
680	def sections_of(view: View) -> list[tuple[int, int, str]]:
681	    """Partition the WHOLE view into (start, end, section). Every character is assigned — 100%."""
682	    text = view.text
683	    bounds: list[tuple[int, str]] = []
684	    start = 0
685	    for m in re.finditer(r'\n', text):
686	        ln = text[start:m.start()]
687	        sec = _classify_heading(ln)
688	        if sec:
689	            bounds.append((start, sec))
690	        start = m.end()
691	    sec = _classify_heading(text[start:])
692	    if sec:
693	        bounds.append((start, sec))
694	
695	    if not bounds:
696	        return [(0, len(text), 'body')]
697	
698	    out: list[tuple[int, int, str]] = []
699	    if bounds[0][0] > 0:
700	        out.append((0, bounds[0][0], 'front'))
701	    for k, (b, sec) in enumerate(bounds):
702	        end = bounds[k + 1][0] if k + 1 < len(bounds) else len(text)
703	        if end > b:
704	            out.append((b, end, sec))
705	    # once a reference list opens, everything after it is bibliography unless an appendix follows
706	    return out
707	
708	
709	def chunk_document(doc_id: str, source: str, size: int = 5200, overlap: int = 600) -> tuple[View, list[Chunk]]:
710	    view = View(source)
711	    text = view.text
712	    if not text.strip():
713	        return view, []
714	
715	    secs = sections_of(view)
716	    tables = find_table_blocks(view)
717	
718	    def sec_at(p: int) -> tuple[str, float]:
719	        for a, b, name in secs:
720	            if a <= p < b:
721	                return name, SECTION_WEIGHT.get(name, 0.5)
722	        return 'body', SECTION_WEIGHT['body']
723	
724	    pieces: list[tuple[int, int, str]] = []       # (start, end, section)
725	    covered: list[tuple[int, int]] = []
726	    for a, b in tables:
727	        name, _ = sec_at(a)
728	        if SECTION_WEIGHT.get(name, 0.5) <= 0:    # a "table" inside the reference list is not a table
729	            continue
730	        # CLIP the block to its own section. A table that runs past the section boundary would
731	        # otherwise be counted under two sections at once, and the coverage arithmetic — which is
732	        # the headline claim of this module — would print 100.4% of the text examined.
733	        sec_end = next((e for s, e, nm in secs if s <= a < e), b)
734	        b = min(b, sec_end)
735	        if b - a >= 40:
736	            pieces.append((a, b, 'tables'))
737	            covered.append((a, b))
738	
739	    # everything not inside a table block, split by section, then windowed
740	    cursor = 0
741	    for a, b, name in secs:
742	        p = a
743	        while p < b:
744	            nxt = next((cs for cs, ce in sorted(covered) if cs >= p and cs < b), None)
745	            end = min(nxt, b) if nxt is not None else b
746	            if end > p:
747	                pieces.append((p, end, name))
748	            if nxt is None:
749	                break
750	            p = next(ce for cs, ce in sorted(covered) if cs == nxt)
751	    del cursor
752	
753	    pieces.sort()
754	    chunks: list[Chunk] = []
755	    n = 0
756	    for a, b, name in pieces:
757	        w = SECTION_WEIGHT.get(name, 0.5)
758	        if name == 'tables':
759	            spans = [(a, b)]
760	        else:
761	            spans = []
762	            p = a
763	            while p < b:
764	                q = min(p + size, b)
765	                if q < b:                          # prefer a paragraph/sentence boundary
766	                    win = text[max(p + size - 900, p):q]
767	                    cut = max(win.rfind('\n'), win.rfind('. '))
768	                    if cut > 200:
769	                        q = max(p + size - 900, p) + cut + 1
770	                spans.append((p, q))
771	                if q >= b:
772	                    break
773	                p = max(q - overlap, p + 1)
774	        for (p, q) in spans:
775	            if q - p < 40:
776	                continue
777	            sa, sb = view.src_span(p, q)
778	            chunks.append(Chunk(doc_id=doc_id, idx=n, section=name, weight=w,
779	                                v_start=p, v_end=q, s_start=sa, s_end=sb, text=text[p:q]))
780	            n += 1
781	    return view, chunks

import json, sys
sys.path.insert(0,'.')
import provenance as P
corpus=json.load(open('../outputs/journal_corpus_content.json'))
g=P.migrate(corpus)
from collections import Counter
print('works',len(g.works),'expr',len(g.expressions),'manif',len(g.manifestations))
c=Counter()
adm_jo=0; adm_any=0; complete=0
for mid,m in g.manifestations.items():
    c[(m.text_field, m.profile['artifact_kind'], m.profile['complete'])]+=1
    if m.profile['complete']: complete+=1
    if g.resolve_attribution(mid,P.JOURNAL_ONLY).admitted: adm_jo+=1
    if g.resolve_attribution(mid,P.ANY_VERSION).admitted: adm_any+=1
for k,v in sorted(c.items(), key=lambda x:-x[1]): print(f'  {v:>3}  field={k[0]:<9} kind={k[1]:<20} complete={k[2]}')
print('COMPLETE manifestations:',complete)
print('admitted JOURNAL_ONLY :',adm_jo)
print('admitted ANY_VERSION  :',adm_any)
# Acemoglu
for mid,m in g.manifestations.items():
    w=g.works[m.work_id]
    if '705716' in (w.doi or ''):
        a=g.resolve_attribution(mid,P.JOURNAL_ONLY)
        print()
        print('AR manif',mid,'field',m.text_field,'expr',m.expression_id)
        print('  kind:',m.profile['artifact_kind'],'complete',m.profile['complete'])
        print('  JOURNAL_ONLY admitted:',a.admitted)
        print('  text:',a.text)
        print('  refusal:',(a.refusal or '')[:200])
" 2>&1 | tail -30

content.text: Now the miner. First the registry loader and imports.

content.content:
1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""EVIDENCE MINER — full-document, section-aware, tuple-gated evidence extraction.
3	
4	WHY THIS EXISTS
5	---------------
6	`cellcog_composer.extract_cards` reads `fulltext[:28000]`. Measured on this corpus that is 33.5% of
7	the usable text, and it is the WRONG 33.5%: the first 28,000 characters of a journal PDF are the
8	title page, the acknowledgements, the abstract and the introduction. Findings do not live there.
9	RESULTS sections and TABLES live at characters 40,000-100,000, and we have never once looked at them.
10	That is the entire reason 1,825 quantitative sentences sit in fulltext we already hold and 2 reached
11	the page.
12	
13	WHAT THIS DOES DIFFERENTLY
14	--------------------------
15	  1. SECTION-AWARE CHUNKING over 100% of the text. Every character is chunked, classified
16	     (front / abstract / introduction / background / METHODS / RESULTS / TABLES / discussion /
17	     conclusion / appendix / references) and weighted. Results and tables are weighted highest.
18	  2. DETERMINISTIC HARVEST FIRST. Regexes find every sentence carrying an effect size, percentage,
19	     coefficient, elasticity, CI, p-value, sample size, study period or comparative quantity.
20	     This stage is exhaustive, free, and CANNOT HALLUCINATE. It tells the LLM where to look.
21	  3. SEMANTIC EXTRACTION into a COMPLETE ESTIMATE TUPLE — effect, unit, comparator, outcome,
22	     population, geography, period, technology, industry, unit_of_analysis, design, uncertainty.
23	     An orphan number is not a finding and is rejected.
24	  4. THE SPAN IS THE ONLY EVIDENCE.
25	
26	THE THREE HOLES THIS CLOSES BY CONSTRUCTION (not by checking)
27	-------------------------------------------------------------
28	  (a) "60 real chars + an invented tail".  We do not verify the model's span string and then keep it.
29	      We use the model's string ONLY AS A SEARCH KEY, and what we STORE is `source[a:b]` — a literal
30	      slice of the paper. The model's copy is discarded. A span that cannot be located is dropped;
31	      a span that locates is, by definition, the source's own bytes. There is no tail to invent.
32	
33	  (b) THE SUBSTRING LEAK.  `"0.2" in "10.25"` is True in Python, so a fabricated effect size used to
34	      pass whenever the source happened to contain a longer number containing its digits. We never
35	      substring-test a number. We TOKENIZE both sides into whole numbers and compare canonical
36	      numeric values, so 0.2 is not found in a span whose only number is 10.25. (test_substring_leak)
37	
38	  (c) EVIDENCE LAUNDERING.  The old extractor asked the model to "state the finding IN YOUR WORDS"
39	      and the gate then validated the writing against those words. THE MODEL WAS VALIDATED AGAINST
40	      ITSELF. Here the model is NEVER ASKED FOR A CLAIM. There is no model-authored free-text summary
41	      field anywhere in the schema. `claim` is a DISPLAY CACHE, composed by this module from tuple
42	      fields that have ALREADY been gated against the verbatim span, and nothing is ever validated
43	      against it. The laundering path does not exist to be re-opened.
44	
45	PROVENANCE, HONESTLY
46	--------------------
47	An effect and its unit must be IN THE SPAN. But a study's GEOGRAPHY and PERIOD are usually stated in
48	the methods section, not in the sentence that carries the coefficient. Forcing them into the span
49	would either starve the tuple or tempt the model to pad the span — which corrupts the only evidence
50	we have. So each field has a declared PROVENANCE WINDOW of VERBATIM SOURCE TEXT (span < chunk <
51	paper), and every card records which window each field came from. The window is widened over
52	*verbatim text from the same paper*. It is NEVER widened to model prose.
53	
54	And regardless of window: EVERY FIGURE IN EVERY FIELD MUST APPEAR IN THE SPAN, as its own number.
55	A number may not enter a card from a wider window than the sentence that is quoted for it.
56	
57	    python scripts/evidence_miner.py --dry-run            # deterministic harvest only, no LLM, free
58	    python scripts/evidence_miner.py --self-test          # the adversarial suite
59	    python scripts/evidence_miner.py                      # full mine -> outputs/evidence_cards_v2.json
60	"""
61	from __future__ import annotations
62	
63	import argparse
64	import concurrent.futures as futures
65	import hashlib
66	import json
67	import os
68	import re
69	import sys
70	import threading
71	import time
72	from dataclasses import dataclass, field, asdict
73	from pathlib import Path
74	
75	ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
76	sys.path.insert(0, str(ROOT))
77	sys.path.insert(0, str(ROOT / 'scripts'))
78	
79	CORPUS = ROOT / 'outputs' / 'journal_corpus_content.json'
80	OUT_CARDS = ROOT / 'outputs' / 'evidence_cards_v2.json'
81	OUT_META = ROOT / 'outputs' / 'evidence_cards_v2.meta.json'
82	CONTRACT_JSON = ROOT / 'outputs' / 'research_contract.json'
83	
84	MODEL = os.getenv('PG_MINER_MODEL') or os.getenv('PG_GENERATOR_MODEL', 'z-ai/glm-5.2')
85	
86	_print_lock = threading.Lock()
87	
88	
89	def log(msg: str) -> None:
90	    with _print_lock:
91	        print(msg, flush=True)
92	
93	
94	# =============================================================================================
95	# 0. THE RESEARCH CONTRACT.  Owned by research_contract.py (another agent). Imported DEFENSIVELY:

_print_lock = threading.Lock()


def log(msg: str) -> None:
    with _print_lock:
        print(msg, flush=True)


def prov():
    """provenance.py, imported LAZILY — it does `from evidence_miner import View, sections_of,
    SECTION_WEIGHT` at ITS top, so a top-level import here is a circular one that fails at line 79,
    before `View` exists. Deferring it to first call means this module's body is complete by then.

    THE GRAPH IS NOT OPTIONAL. This accessor exists to resolve an import cycle, NOT to make provenance
    a soft dependency: `gate_card()` takes the graph as a REQUIRED keyword argument and a card cannot
    be constructed without one. That is the difference between this and the 6,463 lines of correct,
    self-tested, never-imported modules that let the P0 ship.
    """
    global _PROV
    if _PROV is None:
        import provenance as _p          # noqa: PLC0415 — deliberately deferred; see above
        _PROV = _p
    return _PROV


_PROV = None

content.text: Now replace the hardcoded tuple schema with the registry-derived one.

input.old_string:
# =============================================================================================
# 5. THE TUPLE + ITS GATE
# =============================================================================================

TUPLE_FIELDS = ['effect', 'unit', 'comparator', 'outcome', 'population', 'geography', 'period',
                'technology', 'industry', 'unit_of_analysis', 'design', 'uncertainty']

# Where each field is allowed to have come from. The window is always VERBATIM SOURCE TEXT of the
# same paper — never model prose. The effect and its unit must be in the quoted sentence itself;
# a study's geography and period may legitimately be stated in its methods section.
FIELD_WINDOWS = {
    'effect':      ('span',),
    'unit':        ('span',),
    'uncertainty': ('span',),
    'comparator':  ('span', 'chunk'),
    'outcome':     ('span', 'chunk'),
    'population':  ('span', 'chunk', 'paper'),
    'geography':   ('span', 'chunk', 'paper'),
    'period':      ('span', 'chunk', 'paper'),
    'technology':  ('span', 'chunk', 'paper'),
    'industry':    ('span', 'chunk', 'paper'),
}
LEXICAL_THRESH = 0.6          # same bar as the mechanism gate that closed the 43%-invention hole

# The tuple is COMPLETE when it is interpretable on its own: a magnitude, in a unit, of a named
# outcome, for a stated population, from a stated design, in a stated scope. That is the objective —
# NOT a count of numbers. cellcog's "202 findings" include years and sample sizes.
CORE = ('effect', 'unit', 'outcome')
SCOPE = ('population', 'geography', 'period', 'industry', 'technology')
input.new_string:
# =============================================================================================
# 5. TYPED EVIDENCE ACTS + THEIR GATE
# =============================================================================================
#
# THE EXTRACTOR USED TO KNOW ONE KIND OF EVIDENCE: a quantitative tuple. Everything else was silently
# destroyed — `if kind == 'qualitative' and not fields['outcome']: return None` — and a judicial
# opinion, whose evidence is a HOLDING and has no effect size, no population and no design, produced
# ZERO CARDS. The discard was not even counted. That was built to chase D1, which has weight 0.014:
# moving it 5.90 -> 10.00 is worth about +0.0057 of scalar, and it was paid for with every qualitative
# source in the world.
#
# So the schema is now a REGISTRY, IN DATA (config/evidence_acts.json). Adding an act type to a corpus
# of case law is a DATA EDIT. Nothing below names an act, a field or a required-field rule: the names,
# the fields, the windows and the rules are all READ.
#
# D1 CANNOT FALL. `quantitative_estimate` in the registry reproduces the old gate exactly — an effect,
# a number that is in the span, and one of unit/comparator/outcome (the old GATE 6 orphan rule) — the
# full-document scan is untouched, the numeric gates below are untouched, and the qualitative acts are
# ADDITIVE: no cap anywhere lets one displace a quantitative act.

class ActRegistryError(RuntimeError):
    """The act registry is missing or self-inconsistent. THE MINER DOES NOT RUN WITHOUT IT.

    There is no default schema baked into this file to fall back on. A fallback is how a data registry
    becomes decoration: the code keeps working when the data is wrong, so nobody finds out it is wrong.
    """


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class EvidenceAct:
    id: str
    legacy_card_kind: str            # 'estimate' | 'projection' | 'qualitative' — the v1 surface
    label: str
    when: str
    fields: tuple[str, ...]
    required_all: tuple[str, ...]
    required_any: tuple[tuple[str, ...], ...]
    requires_number_in_span: bool
    requires_result_verb: bool
    tuple_bearing: bool              # does `complete_tuple` (the D1 evidence table) apply?


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ActRegistry:
    version: str
    fields: dict[str, dict]          # name -> {windows, prompt, enum?}
    acts: dict[str, EvidenceAct]

    @property
    def field_names(self) -> list[str]:
        return list(self.fields)

    def windows(self, fname: str) -> tuple[str, ...]:
        return tuple(self.fields[fname].get('windows') or ('span',))


def load_act_registry(path: Path = ACT_REGISTRY) -> ActRegistry:
    """Read the registry and CHECK IT AGAINST ITSELF. An act that requires a field it does not declare,
    or declares a field the field table does not define, is a schema that cannot gate anything."""
    try:
        raw = json.loads(path.read_text())
    except Exception as e:
        raise ActRegistryError(f'cannot read the evidence-act registry at {path}: '
                               f'{type(e).__name__}: {e}') from e
    fields = raw.get('fields') or {}
    acts_raw = raw.get('acts') or []
    if not fields or not acts_raw:
        raise ActRegistryError(f'{path} declares no fields and/or no acts')

    acts: dict[str, EvidenceAct] = {}
    for a in acts_raw:
        aid = a.get('id') or ''
        af = tuple(a.get('fields') or ())
        unknown = [f for f in af if f not in fields]
        if unknown:
            raise ActRegistryError(f'act {aid!r} declares field(s) {unknown} that the registry\'s '
                                   f'field table does not define')
        req_all = tuple(a.get('required_all') or ())
        req_any = tuple(tuple(grp) for grp in (a.get('required_any') or ()))
        for f in list(req_all) + [f for grp in req_any for f in grp]:
            if f not in af:
                raise ActRegistryError(f'act {aid!r} REQUIRES field {f!r} but does not declare it — a '
                                       f'required field that is never extracted rejects every card')
        lck = a.get('legacy_card_kind') or 'qualitative'
        if lck not in ('estimate', 'projection', 'qualitative'):
            raise ActRegistryError(f'act {aid!r}: legacy_card_kind {lck!r} is not one of '
                                   f'estimate|projection|qualitative (the v1 surface downstream reads)')
        acts[aid] = EvidenceAct(
            id=aid, legacy_card_kind=lck, label=a.get('label') or aid, when=a.get('when') or '',
            fields=af, required_all=req_all, required_any=req_any,
            requires_number_in_span=bool(a.get('requires_number_in_span')),
            requires_result_verb=bool(a.get('requires_result_verb')),
            tuple_bearing=bool(a.get('tuple_bearing')))
    if not any(x.requires_number_in_span for x in acts.values()):
        raise ActRegistryError('no act in the registry requires a number in its span — the quantitative '
                               'lane (D1) has been deleted from the schema')
    return ActRegistry(version=str(raw.get('registry_version') or '?'), fields=fields, acts=acts)


REGISTRY = load_act_registry()

#: DERIVED from the registry, never maintained beside it. Two lists of fields will drift, and the one
#: that drifts is always the one a gate reads.
TUPLE_FIELDS = REGISTRY.field_names

# Where each field is allowed to have come from. The window is always VERBATIM SOURCE TEXT of the
# same paper — never model prose. The effect and its unit must be in the quoted sentence itself;
# a study's geography and period may legitimately be stated in its methods section.
FIELD_WINDOWS = {f: REGISTRY.windows(f) for f in TUPLE_FIELDS}

LEXICAL_THRESH = 0.6          # same bar as the mechanism gate that closed the 43%-invention hole

#: The act a model proposes for a span that ALSO carries no act — and the act every legacy caller and
#: every legacy card means when it says `card_kind: 'estimate'`.
DEFAULT_ACT = 'quantitative_estimate'

# The tuple is COMPLETE when it is interpretable on its own: a magnitude, in a unit, of a named
# outcome, for a stated population, from a stated design, in a stated scope. That is the objective —
# NOT a count of numbers. cellcog's "202 findings" include years and sample sizes.
CORE = ('effect', 'unit', 'outcome')
SCOPE = ('population', 'geography', 'period', 'industry', 'technology')

def harvest(chunk: Chunk, contract: MiningContract) -> list[dict]:
    """Every sentence in the chunk that carries a quantity. NO LLM. This is the recall stage; the
    tuple gate downstream is the precision stage."""
    if chunk.weight <= 0:
        return []          # a bibliography is dense with numbers and contains no evidence
    cands: list[dict] = []
    for s, e, txt in sentences(chunk.text, base=chunk.v_start):
        t = txt.strip()
        if len(t) < 25 or len(t) > 1200:
            continue
        if not re.search(r'\d', t):
            continue
        if NOISE.search(t):
            continue
        kinds = sorted(k for k, pat in QUANT.items() if pat.search(t))
        if not kinds:
            continue
        # an orphan number with no words around it is a page number, not a finding
        if len(content_words(t)) < 3:
            continue
        verbs = bool(RESULT_VERB.search(t))
        fh = contract.n_tags(t)
        score = chunk.weight * (1.0 + 0.35 * len(kinds)) * (1.6 if verbs else 1.0) * (1.0 + 0.25 * min(fh, 4))
        cands.append({'v_start': s, 'v_end': e, 'text': flatten(t), 'kinds': kinds,
                      'result_verb': verbs, 'facet_hits': fh, 'score': round(score, 3),
                      'section': chunk.section})
    return cands
